op.name: array_split
op.notes: Split array into sub-arrays (possibly unequal). Cost: numel(output).
op.summary: Split an array into multiple sub-arrays.

blocks.text: Split an array into multiple sub-arrays.

Please refer to the ``split`` documentation.  The only difference
between these functions is that ``array_split`` allows
`indices_or_sections` to be an integer that does *not* equally
divide the axis. For an array of length l that should be split
into n sections, it returns l % n sub-arrays of size l//n + 1
and the rest of size l//n.

blocks.code:
>>> import numpy as np
>>> x = np.arange(8.0)
>>> np.array_split(x, 3)
[array([0.,  1.,  2.]), array([3.,  4.,  5.]), array([6.,  7.])]

>>> x = np.arange(9)
>>> np.array_split(x, 4)
[array([0, 1, 2]), array([3, 4]), array([5, 6]), array([7, 8])]

blocks.text: split — Split array into multiple sub-arrays of equal size.
